dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 4 responding for Headache call at Healing Place, Apt 2.
Hi ma'am, I'm John, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
I have a really bad headache, and I feel dizzy. It happened all of a sudden.
Ma'am, can you tell me your name and today's date?
Karen. Tuesday, September 3rd?
Look at this light - what city are we in?
[CITY].
Can you squeeze my fingers, one hand at a time?
Sure, okay.
Ma'am, I see your airway is clear and you're breathing well.
Similar if not equal chest expansion. Lung sounds are good and equal on both sides. Breathing has adequate depth. Respiration rate is 16.
Pulse is around 92, strong and regular. Your SpO₂ is 97%.
Pulse: 92.
Respirations: 16.
Blood pressure: 94 over 73.
Blood sugar is 105.
SpO₂ standing at 97%.
EKG shows sinus rhythm.
I'll place some pads on your chest. We'll check a 4-lead EKG now.
Monitor shows sinus rhythm. Let's now do a full 12-lead for more information.
Karen, when did the headache start and tell me more about it?

I was just sitting down when it came on strong. I've never had anything like this before. 23. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E: samples; Paramedic: Did you have any other symptoms with it like blurred vision or ringing in your ears? 24. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E: samples; Patient: No, just the pain and feeling off-balance. 25. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E: allergies; Paramedic: Do you have any allergies to medicines or other substances? 26. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E: samples; Patient: Doesn't seem so, no. I do take metformin for my type two diabetes. 27. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E: medications; Paramedic: And that's with or without other meds like Glypizide? 28. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E: samples; Patient: Sometimes I take Glypizide depending on blood sugar. No other meds or supplements.
What is your past medical history? 30. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E: samples; Patient: Type 2 diabetes. I had trichomoniasis some time ago.
When was the last time you ate or drank anything?
I haven't had anything since yesterday.
When exactly did the pain first begin, ma'am? 34. Pain Assessment; O.P.Q.R.S.T: samples; Patient: Around 11:00 AM.

Can you describe the nature of the headache? Is it thumping, sharp, or something else? 36. Pain Assessment; O.P.Q.R.S.T: samples; Patient: Like pressure around the head, a tight band wrapping me, along with dizziness.
On a scale from 0 to 10, how bad is this pain right now? 38. Pain Assessment; O.P.Q.R.S.T: samples; Patient: 10. It's constant, no improvement.
Let me look at your skin - is it warm or cool to the touch?
It feels warm. No swelling in the legs, skin is dry.
Karen, can I check your pupils and ears with a light?
Sure, go ahead.
Pupils are equal and reactive at about 3 mm. Trachea is midline. No signs of jugular vein distention.
Can you tell me where you are and what day it is?
I'm in a clinic in [CITY]. It's Tuesday today.
Have you noticed any recent infections or bite marks?
No, nothing like that.
I'm going to check your abdomen lightly. Does it feel normal or tender?
Feels soft. No pain with light touch.
Arms and legs - can you move them without difficulty?
Yes. Arms are balanced. I can pick up things fine.
Her arms and legs move equally with no drift. Pelvis is stable with no swelling. Abdomen is non-tender. Gait is normal with no disconjugate gaze. Equal grip strength in both hands.
Let me rest check the vitals. BP is 139 over 90.
Resp is 16, SpO₂ is 99% now.
Pulse still at 92.
Resp remains at 16.
Blood pressure is 139 over 90.
Glucose is 0 using a lateral method.
SpO₂ is 99%. Oxygen saturation is steady.
EKG still shows sinus rhythm with no major changes.
We've done another 12-lead EKG as part of protocol.
We're following Stroke/TIA protocol. No immediate treatment is needed.
We are en route to [COMPANY_NAME] Triage for evaluation.
Karen, any medical insurance cards or insurance information available?
I have Medicaid and Humana, but no cards. The Healing Place doesn't have my info either.
We'll help you up to the stretcher next to the clinic.
Hospital staff are ready for your arrival.
She's now on the hospital bed. No new symptoms, consistent with the last assessment.
Full report provided to the RN on duty.
Appreciate the thorough report. Keep up the great work out there.
Karen seems alert and oriented to person, place, time, and event - awake and aware.
Do you have any problems with balance or coordination?
No, I feel stable on my feet. No issues there.
No jugular vein distention noted during exam. Would you confirm?
Yep, no JVD - all per the narrative.
